report_sections.section_text: the waking background shows fair organization, consisting of predominantly 20-40 v, 9 hz posterior background activity with good reactivity. there is marked  beta activity bilaterally. there is moderate theta slowing bilaterally,  especially during sleep. intermittent drowsiness is characterized by attenuation  of the background, slow roving eye movements and bilateral slowing in the  theta and delta range with shifting predominance. sleep is characterized  by, further slowing, vertex waves, sleep spindles and k-complexes. hyperventilation  is not performed. photic stimulation results in marked driving bilaterally.   single lead ekg shows an apparent sinus rhythm of approximately 70 beats  per minute.    this routine eeg, performed in the waking, drowsy and  sleeping states, is abnormal due to marked theta slowing and  bilateral slowing. these findings are consistent with a  prior diagnosis of focal seizures with impaired awareness.  there are no definite focal or epileptiform abnormalities.  there are no prior eegs available for comparison. ***** eeg is within normal limits with regard to seizure focus.  ***** eeg is within normal limits with regard to seizure focus.
